title: Deep Dissection of Hand
structures: superficial and deep branches of ulnar nerve
topic: deep musculature and neurovasculature of the hand
description: A detailed deep dissection drawing of both the palmar and dorsal aspects of the hand. It highlights the intricate arrangement of the intrinsic thumb muscles (thenar group), the deep flexor tendons, and the precise pathways of the median nerve and branches of the ulnar nerve in the palm.
clinical_notes: Lacerations to the palmar surface can easily sever the superficial and deep branches of the ulnar nerve or the median nerve. OT rehabilitation post-repair focuses on early protected mobilization using dorsal blocking splints to prevent tendon adhesions while safeguarding the healing nerve and tendon anastomoses.
openstax_chapter: Chapter 11 – The Muscular System & Chapter 13 – The Peripheral Nervous System
